title: To A Redbreast (In Sickness)
content:
Stay, little cheerful Robin! stay,
 And at my casement sing,
 Though it should prove a farewell lay
 And this our parting spring.

 Though I, alas! may ne'er enjoy
 The promise in thy song;
 A charm, 'that' thought can not destroy,
 Doth to thy strain belong.

 Methinks that in my dying hour
 Thy song would still be dear,
 And with a more than earthly power
 My passing Spirit cheer.

 Then, little Bird, this boon confer,
 Come, and my requiem sing,
 Nor fail to be the harbinger
 Of everlasting Spring.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Wordsworth delivers a powerful performance in "To A Redbreast (In Sickness)"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
